page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 physical cores on the ARMv9 architecture using the Neoverse-V3 model, operating at 2.8 GHz with a dedicated CPU allocation and no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance is equipped with 11400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with the stress-ng multi-core test and PassMark CPU score ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also highly competitive, achieving top-tier status in both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached read and write operations. However, single-core performance represents a notable tradeoff, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and LLM prompt processing for small models scoring in the average tier. Furthermore, LLM text generation speed for a small 135M model is categorized in the bottom 10%, though prompt processing for a medium 7B model remains in the top tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m9gd.48xlarge offers high resource density by combining a large physical core count with substantial local NVMe storage and high-speed networking on a power-efficient ARM architecture. This profile makes the instance suitable for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and high-throughput static web serving, where its top-tier PassMark database operations and Redis SET performance can be fully leveraged. It is less suited for workloads dependent on high single-threaded speed or intensive LLM text generation.
description: The Amazon Web Services m9gd.48xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 768.0 GB of RAM, 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside average single-core performance. While LLM text generation speeds are relatively low, the instance delivers high throughput for multi-threaded applications, Redis deployments, and static web serving. This hardware profile offers a balanced resource density, combining high core counts with massive local storage on an energy-efficient ARM architecture.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.48xlarge is an ARM64 instance featuring 192 Graviton physical cores, 768 GB RAM, 11400 GB NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier multi-core and database benchmark performance.

tagline: An ARM64 instance combining high physical core density, massive local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-core performance for data-intensive workloads.

768.0 GB of system memory and 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark results

Suited for multi-threaded applications, Redis, and high-throughput web serving
